notice: Macro Regime Scanner v0.24 input registry. Adds BLS CPI, core CPI, PPI, unemployment, payroll, and wage public-source macro inputs.

inputs.name: 10Y yield
derived_examples: 10-year government bond yield trend and level

inputs.name: 2Y yield
derived_examples: 2-year government bond yield trend and level

inputs.name: Breakeven inflation
derived_examples: 5Y and 10Y breakeven inflation

inputs.name: CPI pressure
derived_examples: CPI YoY, CPI MoM, Core CPI, and actual-vs-forecast surprise

inputs.name: Credit spreads
source_candidates: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
derived_examples: High-yield and investment-grade spreads vs history

inputs.name: Crop progress / condition
derived_examples: Planting, emergence, harvest progress, and good/excellent crop ratings

inputs.name: Crude inventories
derived_examples: Weekly U.S. commercial crude oil stock change

inputs.name: Cushing inventories
derived_examples: Cushing crude stock level and weekly change

inputs.name: Ethanol / biofuel linkage
derived_examples: EIA/USDA ethanol production, corn use for ethanol, and biofuel-demand data

inputs.name: Export sales
derived_examples: Weekly export sales and shipments

inputs.name: Financial conditions
source_candidates: FRED / Federal Reserve public-source candidate with series rights check
derived_examples: Financial conditions index or composite of rates, dollar, equities, and credit

inputs.name: GDP / growth trend
derived_examples: GDP growth, nowcast, or growth surprise vs prior/forecast

inputs.name: Global supply competitors
derived_examples: Brazil/Argentina/Black Sea/EU/Australia supply where relevant

inputs.name: Imports / exports
derived_examples: Petroleum or commodity trade flow changes

inputs.name: LNG exports / power burn
derived_examples: LNG export flows and gas used for electricity generation

inputs.name: Labor strength
derived_examples: NFP, unemployment rate, claims, wages, JOLTS, and revisions

inputs.name: Natural gas storage
derived_examples: Weekly gas storage change and storage vs 5-year average

inputs.name: OPEC policy
derived_examples: OPEC official production targets, cuts, increases, compliance notes, and public monthly releases

inputs.name: Open interest
derived_examples: Futures open interest trend and change

inputs.name: PCE pressure
derived_examples: PCE and Core PCE inflation readings

inputs.name: PPI pressure
derived_examples: PPI YoY, PPI MoM, and producer-price surprise

inputs.name: Policy rate
derived_examples: Current central bank target rate or policy band

inputs.name: Product inventories
derived_examples: Gasoline and distillate stock changes

inputs.name: Product supplied / demand
derived_examples: EIA product supplied for gasoline, distillates, and total products

inputs.name: Rate differentials
derived_examples: Relevant yield spread between countries or curves, usually 2Y and 10Y comparisons

inputs.name: Real yield differentials
derived_examples: Inflation-adjusted yield spread, often 10Y real yield vs peer real yield

inputs.name: Real yields
derived_examples: 10Y TIPS real yield trend and level

inputs.name: Refinery utilization
derived_examples: Percent of refinery capacity utilized

inputs.name: Retail sales / consumer demand
derived_examples: Retail sales headline/control group and surprise

inputs.name: Stock/use ratio
derived_examples: Ending stocks divided by total use

inputs.name: U.S. production
derived_examples: U.S. crude or natural gas production estimate

inputs.name: WASDE ending stocks
derived_examples: USDA WASDE ending stocks for wheat, corn, soybeans, cotton

inputs.name: Weather / drought
derived_examples: NOAA/NWS weather, drought monitor, rainfall, heat/freeze risk, and crop-region conditions where available

inputs.name: Weather HDD/CDD
derived_examples: Heating-degree days, cooling-degree days, and public weather/forecast deviations where available

inputs.name: Yield curve
derived_examples: 2Y minus 10Y, 5Y minus 30Y, or comparable curve slope

inputs.name: COT spec direction
derived_examples: Managed Money or Leveraged Funds net positioning as a share of open interest

inputs.name: COT spec weekly change
derived_examples: Weekly change in speculative net exposure

inputs.name: COT crowding risk
derived_examples: Speculative net positioning percentile/range within recent lookback

inputs.name: COT commercial / hedger extreme
derived_examples: Producer/Merchant/Processor/User net positioning percentile for commodity futures

inputs.name: COT spec-commercial conflict
derived_examples: Opposition between speculative and commercial/hedger net positioning

inputs.name: USDA crop condition
derived_examples: Good/excellent crop condition percentages and poor/very-poor ratings

inputs.name: USDA production estimate
derived_examples: National crop production estimates and year-over-year changes

inputs.name: USDA crop progress context
derived_examples: Planting, emergence, harvest, and other crop progress percentages

inputs.name: BLS CPI pressure
inputs.definition: Consumer inflation pressure from BLS CPI all-items index. Hot CPI usually supports inflation/rate pressure and can pressure risk assets through tighter-policy concerns.

inputs.name: BLS core CPI pressure
inputs.definition: Underlying consumer inflation pressure from CPI less food and energy. Sticky core CPI usually supports policy-rate pressure.

inputs.name: BLS PPI pressure
inputs.definition: Producer-price pressure from BLS PPI final demand. Hot PPI can feed cost and margin pressure.

inputs.name: BLS unemployment rate
inputs.definition: Labor-market slack from BLS unemployment rate. Rising unemployment weakens labor/growth pressure and can raise recession risk.

inputs.name: BLS payroll growth
inputs.definition: Monthly nonfarm payroll change. Strong payrolls support growth/labor strength but can keep rate pressure firmer.

inputs.name: BLS wage pressure
inputs.definition: Average hourly earnings wage pressure. Strong wages support incomes but can keep services inflation sticky.
